summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Caddy's http_redirect listener wrapper reflects the attacker-controlled Host header directly into the Location response header, creating an open redirect from HTTPS URLs to arbitrary attacker-controlled hosts.
expected_security_impact: Sending an HTTP request with Host: evil.com to the TLS port should produce a 308 Permanent Redirect to Location: https://evil.com/test-path, demonstrating that the Host header is reflected without validation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built Caddy from source, started it with the http_redirect listener wrapper, and sent a plaintext HTTP request with Host: evil.com to the TLS port. The server responded with a 308 Permanent Redirect to Location: https://evil.com/test-path, confirming the Host header is reflected without validation.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts Caddy with the http_redirect listener wrapper, sends a crafted HTTP request using curl with a Host: evil.com header to the TLS port, captures the response headers, and checks whether the Location header contains 'https://evil.com'. The check distinguishes a genuine open redirect from a normal server response or error.
evidence_produced_by_run: The Location response header contains 'https://evil.com/test-path' reflecting the attacker-controlled Host header.
The 308 Permanent Redirect status code confirms the redirect is treated as permanent by Caddy.
The EVOHUNT_PROOF_STRING OPEN_REDIRECT_HOST_HEADER_REFLECTED was emitted by the verification script.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the master branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
